Research tools return no directly useful results here — the domain knowledge (Meisl/Knowles amyloid master equation, Cohen 2013) is well established: inhibitors act by (i) reducing the secondary nucleation rate constant (Langmuir surface binding) and (ii) lengthening the effective lag time, both recovering baseline at cd=0. That's exactly what the 0.9586 pool programs encode.

My parent (0.9545) has a distinct, more compact structure. Rather than converting it into the pool's family (would lose diversity value), I'll make **targeted improvements** that address its two clear weaknesses:

1. **The linear time drive `c[10]*time*capacity`** is a crude lag mechanism — it pushes growth at all times equally, giving an inflated slope near t=0 rather than a proper flat lag. Replacing it with a smooth **saturating time-gate** on the autocatalytic term (rises from 0→1 with an inhibitor-lengthened half-time) directly targets the shape loss / crossing-time terms while preserving cd=0 behavior.
